# đź—‚ Taxonomy

This page defines the reader-facing taxonomy used across the repository. It is a reference page, not a theory essay.

## Research Stage Taxonomy

### Macro research-stage map

| Stage | What it captures |
| --- | --- |
| `Discover & Synthesize` | Literature search, evidence gathering, contradiction spotting, and structured synthesis. |
| `Ideate` | Hypothesis generation, gap finding, analogical search, and research framing. |
| `Plan & Design` | Protocol drafting, experiment planning, evaluation design, and task decomposition. |
| `Implement` | Code generation, pipeline assembly, environment setup, and artifact construction. |
| `Execute & Experiment` | Running experiments, tool orchestration, adaptive retries, and lab-in-the-loop execution. |
| `Analyze & Visualize` | Result interpretation, figure generation, statistical inspection, and error analysis. |
| `Write & Review` | Drafting manuscripts, assembling reports, review simulation, and revision. |

### Fine-grained stage legend

- **Discover & Synthesize**: `literature search`, `retrieval and ranking`, `evidence synthesis`, `contradiction detection`
- **Ideate**: `hypothesis generation`, `gap analysis`, `analogy mining`, `novelty checking`
- **Plan & Design**: `study design`, `experiment planning`, `evaluation planning`, `tool selection`
- **Implement**: `code scaffolding`, `pipeline composition`, `data preparation`, `tool integration`
- **Execute & Experiment**: `experiment execution`, `tool orchestration`, `adaptive retries`, `physical experiment handoff`
- **Analyze & Visualize**: `result analysis`, `visualization`, `ablation reading`, `failure analysis`
- **Write & Review**: `paper drafting`, `report assembly`, `review simulation`, `revision`

## Role Taxonomy

| Level | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| `L1 Tools` | Human dominates the loop. The AI acts as a tool, local component, or narrow assistant rather than as a workflow owner. |
| `L2 Collaborators` | Human in the loop. The AI can advance multi-step work, but humans still steer, gate, or validate important decisions. |
| `L3 Systems` | AI dominates the loop. The system owns substantial execution and iteration, while humans mainly provide goals, constraints, or downstream review. |

`self-evolving` is tracked as a tag; see [Self-Evolving Systems](views/self-evolving.md).

## Application Domain Taxonomy

| Domain | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| `Artificial Intelligence` | Applications in artificial intelligence research, machine learning research workflows, and AI-for-AI systems with explicit AI research targets or evaluation. |
| `Biomedical` | Applications in biology, medicine, drug discovery, and biomedical literature. |
| `Chemistry` | Applications in chemical reasoning, synthesis, and molecular discovery. |
| `Computer Science` | Applications in non-AI computer science research such as formal methods, programming systems, and algorithmic reasoning. |
| `General` | Cross-domain or discipline-agnostic systems for AI-driven research workflows, literature work, agent infrastructure, and research methodology that are not clearly anchored to one vertical field. |
| `Materials Science` | Applications in materials discovery, materials property reasoning, and experimental design. |
| `Math` | Applications in mathematical reasoning, theorem proving, and formal proof discovery. |
| `Physics` | Applications in physical science reasoning, modeling, and scientific analysis. |
| `Social Science` | Applications in social simulation, policy analysis, behavioral science, and computational social experiments. |

## Benchmark Taxonomy

| Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `System Benchmark` | Measures end-to-end AI research systems or realistic multi-step workflows. |
| `Scientific Reasoning` | Measures scientific problem solving, expert analysis, or domain reasoning quality. |
| `Ideation & Discovery` | Measures hypothesis generation, novelty, or open-ended scientific discovery quality. |

## Human-Role Taxonomy

| Human role | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| `Human-driven` | The human researcher remains the main decision-maker and executor. |
| `Human-in-the-loop` | The system can advance the workflow, but expert feedback remains part of the main loop. |
| `Human executes physical experiments` | The AI plans and interprets, but people still run the wet-lab or physical step. |
| `Mostly automated` | Most digital research stages can run with limited intervention. |
| `Full automation claim` | The system explicitly claims autonomous operation over the core loop. |

## Evidence-Level Taxonomy

| Evidence | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| `Preprint` | Public manuscript without archival publication yet. |
| `Conference` | Peer-reviewed conference publication or accepted proceedings paper. |
| `Journal` | Journal publication or journal-backed archival version. |
| `Technical report` | Substantial report released by a lab or company. |
| `Organization report` | Project or product documentation released by an organization. |
| `Benchmark release` | A benchmark artifact with public code or data release. |
| `Repo only` | Public repository without a stable paper or report yet. |
